title: Lesson Quiz: Matching Self to Careers
description: Assessment of the concept that Connecting personal traits to career options.

questions.prompt: What does it mean to match self to careers?

choices.text: Connecting personal traits and preferences to possible career options
choices.feedback: Correct. Matching self to careers means comparing personal traits to career possibilities.

choices.text: Choosing a career based only on what friends prefer
choices.feedback: Career choices should be based on personal evidence, not only peer opinions.

choices.text: Ignoring interests and values during career planning
choices.feedback: Interests and values are important when matching self to careers.

choices.text: Selecting a job at random to save time
choices.feedback: Random choice does not reflect careful career matching.

questions.prompt: A good career match can be supported by interests, strengths, values, and work preferences.

choices.feedback: A strong career match should use several personal factors together.
Correct. Career matching works best when students use multiple types of personal evidence.

questions.prompt: A student enjoys solving problems, likes math, and prefers detailed work. Which option best explains how this information helps?

choices.text: It proves the student should never explore any other field
choices.feedback: Self-information helps narrow options, but it does not eliminate all other possibilities.

choices.text: It shows patterns that may connect the student to careers with similar demands
choices.feedback: This is close, but the best answer here is the more direct statement about matching traits to careers.

choices.text: It provides evidence that can help match personal traits to career options
choices.feedback: Correct. Personal traits can help students identify careers that may be a stronger fit.

choices.text: It means salary should be ignored completely
choices.feedback: This lesson focuses on matching self to careers, not ignoring all other considerations.

questions.prompt: Why is it useful to compare yourself with more than one career option?

choices.text: Because one quick guess is always enough
choices.feedback: Students should compare options carefully rather than rely on one quick guess.

choices.text: Because comparing options can help students find better career matches
choices.feedback: This is close, but the strongest answer is the one that directly ties comparison to informed decision-making.

choices.text: Because it removes the need for future reflection
choices.feedback: Career planning still involves reflection over time.

choices.text: Because comparing options supports more informed and thoughtful choices
choices.feedback: Correct. Comparing options helps students make stronger career matches based on evidence.

questions.prompt: Matching self to careers is a process that can involve reflection and adjustment over time.

choices.feedback: Correct. Career matching can change as students learn more about themselves and different careers.
Career matching is not always fixed. It can develop over time with more reflection and information.
